title: T-011 — API: bulk PO creation grouped by default supplier
prompt:
Implement FR-PLAN-006 (PO bulk creation). Endpoint `POST /api/planning/purchase-orders/bulk` accepts:
```
{ items: [{ product_id, quantity }] }
```
Groups items by `supplier_products.is_default=true.supplier_id`, drafts one PO per supplier. Items without a default supplier are returned in `warnings[]` (do not block).

Returns `{ created: [{ po_id, po_number, supplier_id, line_count }], warnings: [{ product_id, reason }] }`.

RED test asserts: 5 items with 3 distinct default suppliers + 1 unassigned → 3 PO drafts + 1 warning. Test must run within 5s for 100 lines (perf hint per §13.4).

pipeline_inputs.description: Bulk PO endpoint that groups inputs by default supplier and emits one PO per group.
pipeline_inputs.details: Process all items in a single transaction. Use one supplier_products query with WHERE product_id = ANY(...) AND is_default = true to fetch all default suppliers in one round trip. For each grouped supplier, call the same insertion path used by T-010 (factor common helper). Status='draft' for bulk-created POs (not auto-submitted). Outbox event 'po.bulk_created' emitted once per bulk run.
acceptance_criteria: Given 5 input items and 3 distinct default suppliers + 1 product without default, when bulk creates run, then 3 POs are returned in `created[]` and 1 entry in `warnings[]`.
Given 100 input items spread across 5 suppliers, when bulk creates run, then total wall time is < 5s on a typical dev DB.
Given any group fails to insert, when transaction rolls back, then NO PO from any group is persisted.
Given `pnpm vitest run src/app/api/planning/purchase-orders/bulk/__tests__/route.test.ts`, when run, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED tests for grouping correctness, warning emission, transactional all-or-nothing.
Add a non-failing perf assertion (test runtime < 5s).
risk_red_lines: Do not silently skip unassigned products — they must surface in warnings.
Do not split into multiple transactions — partial bulk insert is a data-integrity hazard.
Do not reuse PO numbers across the group — each PO gets its own number from T-009.
